title: Why the Incomes Differ
event: Reyes Co. produced 10,000 units and sold 8,000. Fixed overhead per unit under absorption costing is $6. Compute the difference between absorption and variable costing income.

computationPaths.narration: The 2,000 unsold units carry $6 of fixed overhead each INTO inventory under absorption — cost that variable costing already expensed.

memorize.body: Income difference = change in inventory units × fixed OH per unit. Production > sales → absorption higher; production < sales → variable higher.
Sell exactly what you produce and the two incomes match — the difference lives entirely in inventory movement.

questions.prompt: Produced 10,000, sold 8,000, fixed OH $6/unit. Absorption income exceeds variable income by ?

distractors.feedback: Direction flipped. Inventory GREW, so absorption DEFERS fixed cost and reports HIGHER income.
That's ALL the fixed overhead. Only the 2,000 unsold units' share (12,000) is deferred.
